# EX09 Website and Analysis

<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->

## Summary

In this project, we analyzed COMP110 students interest in adding a livestreamed lecture to the course. Using multiple data points from a survey sent out to students. 

## Visualization 

The following graphs provide a visualiztiion of student responses. 

## Analysis 

The histogram conveys that most students have a high interest in livestreamed lectures with the most responses towards 6 and 7. The results emphasize a strong interest and demand for recorded lectures among COMP110 students. 

The bar graph supports the same conclusion, having a negative skew and a concentration of responses around 6 and 7 as well. The data reinforces the interest that COMP110 students have in recorded lectures that can be accessed after class. 

The boxplot once again supports the same conclusion with most means being on the higher end, mostly at 5 and 6 on a scale from 1 to 7. All of the graphs support the idea of implementing recorded lectures as COMP110 students find them effective. 

## Conclusion 

In this assignment, we assessed the idea of implementing recorded lectures to the COMP110 course. To do so, we took the data from multiple questions relating to video recordings, which were "add_livestream", "is_effective", and "pre_lecture_videos". 

The data from each of these sections, as can be seen in each of the graphs, supports the idea that students have a preference for livestreamed lectures. In the histogram, the trend it upwards and concentrated around 6 and 7. The bar graph, which clearly shows the count of responses, also supports a strong preference for recorded lectures. The boxplot gives a visual of all of the data used at the same time. The median for "add_livestream" and "pre_lecture_videos" is 6, and the median for "is_effective" is 5. On a scale from 1 to 7, these are all relatively high. 

Based on the data and analysis performed, students strongly prefer a recorded lecture option. Its implementation would most likely improve accessibility and allow access to confusing content after class time and outside of office hours. To counter the negative effects, such as a decline in lecture attendance, attendance could be graded. This way, students would still have an incentive to attend class, even though they can access the content later. 

## Recommendation 

Considering the data and analysis from above, COMP110 should implement recorded lectures. However, as attendance may decrease, other incentives to attend class should be implemented as well.
